_schema: comfy_spark LoRA catalog v1. Maps a friendly name -> spec for a Tier-1 'drop-in' LTX-2 LoRA: one that only patches the MODEL (loaded with LoraLoaderModelOnly) and can therefore be STACKED freely. comfy_launch.py reads this for --lora <name>[:<strength>] and --list-loras; the tkinter UI reads it to populate the LoRA picker. You can also pass a raw .safetensors URL to --lora directly (no catalog entry needed) — that's the escape hatch for the long tail of community LoRAs. Control / IC-LoRAs (depth, pose, canny, inpaint, ...) are NOT here: they need conditioning wired in and ship as their own presets instead.

_fields.url: direct .safetensors download URL (HF 'resolve/main/...' or any host)
_fields.file: filename to save as under models/loras (must be unique per stack)
_fields.base: compatible base model — must match the preset's model (e.g. ltx2.3-22b). A mismatch is warned about loudly.
_fields.kind: effect | style | camera | motion | edit  (all drop-in/stackable)
_fields.task: t2v | i2v | v2v | any  (what input the LoRA expects; advisory)
_fields.trigger: phrase auto-appended to the prompt unless --no-triggers (omit/empty = none)
_fields.strength: recommended default strength_model (LTX docs: 0.9-1.6, ~1.2-1.4 balanced; keep a stack's combined strength under ~2.0)
_fields.note: human-readable usage hint (shown by --list-loras and as a UI tooltip)
_fields.prompt_example: a ready-to-use starting prompt for this LoRA (do NOT include the trigger — that's auto-appended). Shown by --list-loras and in the UI picker.

transition.note: Morph / transition between subjects or scenes. Works for i2v and first-to-last-frame. Describe the transformation in the prompt; the 'zhuanchang' trigger is auto-appended. Model-card template: [shot+camera]. [subject+scene]. [the transformation in detail]. [lighting/texture/atmosphere].
transition.prompt_example: Close-up shot, soft window light. A young woman stands still, then slowly morphs into an elegant white fox as fur spreads across her face, the camera holding steady. Cinematic, shallow depth of field.

vbvr.note: VBVR image-to-video motion/style effect. Start at 1.0; lower toward 0.8 if it overpowers the source image. (No trigger/prompt documented on the model card; the example below is a generic i2v motion starter.)
vbvr.prompt_example: The subject comes alive with natural motion - hair and fabric drift, subtle weight shifts - the camera holds steady, soft cinematic lighting.

anime2real.note: Restyle anime input toward photoreal. Describe the realistic look in the prompt; ease strength down if faces drift. (No trigger documented; the example is a style-descriptor starter.)
anime2real.prompt_example: photorealistic live-action footage, natural skin texture, realistic lighting and soft shadows, shallow depth of field, cinematic color grade

real2anime.note: Restyle photoreal input toward anime. Pairs well with a style description in the prompt. (No trigger documented; the example is a style-descriptor starter.)
real2anime.prompt_example: anime style, cel-shaded, vivid saturated colors, expressive features, clean hand-drawn 2D linework
